body.objective: Make the mock runtime a real HTTP control-loop client: mint a per-child scoped spawn token and inject control identity env, let a spawned child self-spawn a sub-agent over POST /api/swarms/spawns and poll the child status, and prove it end to end including a control-plane-outage non-fatal path.
constraints: The spawn token is scoped to the lease and spawn tree, minted from a shared HMAC secret in a leaf module.
A control-plane outage is non-fatal: the child exits 0, creates no child, and holds no runtime semaphore permit.
Do not bypass the spawn route with an in-process shortcut.
No real tokens consumed in the e2e; fake/mock runtimes only.
acceptance_criteria: The spawned process env carries DJIMITFLO_CONTROL_URL, a scoped DJIMITFLO_SPAWN_TOKEN, DJIMITFLO_LEASE_ID, DJIMITFLO_SPAWN_TREE_ID, and DJIMITFLO_DEPTH.
A mock root self-spawns a child over HTTP and the child a grandchild with correct worker_lease parentage/tree/depth.
Exactly two HTTP-spawned children are observed in the e2e.
A control-plane-outage e2e asserts the mock exits 0, creates no child, and holds no permit.

body.objective: Bound spawn depth and budget: grant each spawned child a bounded token/wall grant capped per depth inside a tree-wide hard bound, arm allow_nested_spawn cooperatively when depth < depth_budget, and keep a depth_budget_exceeded backstop gate for non-cooperative runtimes.
constraints: Per-depth caps (SPAWN_PER_DEPTH_TOKEN_CAP / SPAWN_PER_DEPTH_WALL_CAP_MS) sit inside the tree-wide hard bound.
A non-cooperative runtime attempting depth + 1 beyond depth_budget is rejected with a gated-out response, not a worker failure.
The depth-floor child is disarmed and runs echo-only.
Do not relax the tree-wide hard budget to accommodate deeper trees.
acceptance_criteria: Each prepared child receives a bounded token and wall grant capped per depth.
Cumulative tree consumption stays within total_token_budget / total_wall_budget_ms.
The depth-floor child is not armed, runs echo-only, exits 0, and creates no great-grandchild.
A depth_budget_exceeded gated-out response is a legitimate terminal state.

body.objective: Authorize spawn mutations with either a user JWT or a scoped spawn token, while keeping root creation operator-only via write:swarm_action.
constraints: A token-only caller can POST /spawns but cannot POST /spawns/root.
No-credential callers receive 401 AUTH_REQUIRED; malformed bearers receive 401 AUTH_INVALID without falling through to spawn-token auth.
Do not broaden root creation permissions.
The spawn token must remain scoped to a lease and spawn tree.
acceptance_criteria: Spawn routes mount with requireAuthOrSpawnToken so a user JWT OR a scoped spawn token authorizes POST /spawns.
POST /spawns/root is operator-only via write:swarm_action; a token-only caller gets 401.
An e2e asserts token-only POST /spawns -> 201, no-header -> 401 AUTH_REQUIRED, malformed bearer -> 401 AUTH_INVALID, and token-only POST /spawns/root -> 401.

body.objective: Inject a spawned child's validated LIVE capability manifest as read-only metadata into its env and assignment packet, granting no new authority.
constraints: Only LIVE capabilities (live_route_allowed) are serialized; a missing or non-live capability is silently skipped, not an error.
The manifest is read-only metadata and grants no new authority.
The manifest returns undefined when empty so no env line is injected for capability-less children.
Do not execute capabilities inside the injection path.
acceptance_criteria: LoopService.buildCapabilityManifest serializes only LIVE capabilities as a compact JSON manifest with id, kind, owner, version, status, risk_ceiling, allowed_actions, forbidden_actions, and required_evidence.
buildNestedSpawnEnv sets DJIMITFLO_CAPABILITIES and writeAssignmentPacket writes a capabilities field carrying the manifest.
The mock runtime logs capabilities=<count> so injection is observable.
An e2e registers a live capability, binds it to the root, runs the maker, and asserts the env line capabilities=1 and the packet carries the manifest.

body.objective: Add claude, gemini, and editor (=cline) as loop runtimes with the same headless contract and default-deny security gates as codex and opencode, and probe their runtime contracts via fake binaries.
constraints: The bypass flag (--dangerously-skip-permissions / -y / --auto-approve true) is added ONLY when RUNTIME_ALLOW_SKIP_PERMISSIONS=true.
Runtime contracts probe --version and the runtime help subcommand and mark drifted with an actionable reason when a required flag is missing.
Real runtime smoke runs are manual/optional; the suite proves the path with fake binaries.
Do not weaken the env allowlist, cwd boundary, or semaphore gates for the new runtimes.
acceptance_criteria: buildRuntimeCommand gains claude/gemini/editor branches with the correct headless args and skip-permissions/model toggles.
getRuntimeContract generalizes to a probe table covering all five real runtimes and marks drifted when the json/cwd/headless flag is missing.

RuntimeContract.runtime, getRuntimeContracts(), fleet pools, runtimeCommandAvailable, WorkerRuntime, tickScheduler, RuntimeKind/VALID_RUNTIMES, the spawn error message, ExecutorKind, and loop input runtime unions are extended.

Unit tests cover the three buildRuntimeCommand branches and the available/drifted/unavailable probes, plus a fake-claude self-spawn e2e proving a non-mock runtime follows the control loop and the spawn route accepts runtime: 'claude'.

body.objective: Harden the parallel-load flake deterministically: retry git worktree add on transient lock errors, isolate proof-run-service tests, and map runtime/worktree failures to a stable error code.
constraints: Lock-class git errors are retried a bounded number of times with backoff; non-lock errors are not retried.
proof-run-service tests use per-test temp git repos and per-test LOOP_WORKTREE_ROOT/LOOP_EVIDENCE_ROOT.
PROOF_RUN_RUNTIME_FAILED maps to a stable 503, never a bare 500 INTERNAL_ERROR.
Do not mask non-transient failures as retriable.
acceptance_criteria: createWorktree retries lock-class git errors (worktree.lock / index.lock / another git process / File exists) up to a bounded number of attempts and re-throws WORKTREE_CREATE_FAILED only after retries exhaust.
proof-run-service tests are isolated per test and deterministic under parallel runs.
PROOF_RUN_RUNTIME_FAILED -> stable 503 in mapProofRunError.
Unit tests cover lock-retry-then-success, non-lock no-retry, and the 503 mapping.

body.objective: Extend the discussions substrate with ordered multi-round turns and a computed-on-read next-speaker selector that returns a hint only and spawns nothing.
constraints: Only one open turn may be pending at a time; replies via parent_turn_id are allowed only to a committed turn.
The next-speaker selector is computed-on-read (no scheduler table); tick returns a hint only and spawns nothing.
Turn writes are gated by create:task and tick by write:swarm_action; reads are open to authenticated.
Non-participants are rejected when metadata.participants is non-empty.
acceptance_criteria: A discussion_turns table (ordered turn_index, nullable parent_turn_id, status CHECK open|committed|superseded, FK cascade) is added to schema.ts and applies on every boot via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS.

DiscussionTurnService provides appendTurn, listTurns, computeNextTurn (round-robin over metadata.participants), and setTurnStatus with valid open->committed|superseded and committed->superseded transitions.
Turn routes POST /:id/turns, GET /:id/turns, POST /:id/tick, PATCH /:id/turns/:turnId are wired with correct permissions and broadcast DISCUSSION_TURN_ADDED / DISCUSSION_TURN_COMMITTED.

A route + service test covers append, non-participant refusal, one-open-turn gate, tick round-robin + awaiting-commit, commit/supersede, reply threading, permission gating, and a computeNextTurn unit test.

body.objective: Register claude, gemini, and editor (cline) as ExecutionEngine executors that inherit the existing tasks-path gates and the established spawn/parse contract, mirroring CodexExecutor.
constraints: Executors register via the open/closed registerExecutor map (no switch) and inherit the existing execute:task + risk-classifier + approval-policy gates.
Skip-permissions / model toggles are controlled by the per-executor *_SKIP_PERMISSIONS env gate (the pre-existing tasks-path posture), not the loop path's runtime allowlist.
Argv reuses the proven buildRuntimeCommand shapes for claude/gemini/editor.
Native per-CLI streaming-event schema mapping is deferred; parsing is best-effort NDJSON + heuristic fallback + exit code.
acceptance_criteria: ClaudeExecutor, GeminiExecutor, and EditorExecutor classes mirror CodexExecutor (bin/timeout/skip env, spawn, SIGTERM->5s->SIGKILL, NDJSON + heuristic-fallback parsing).

buildClaudeArgs / buildGeminiArgs / buildEditorArgs emit claude -p <prompt> --output-format json, gemini -p <prompt> -o json, and cline --json --auto-approve <bool> -c <worktree> --thinking <t> <prompt> respectively, with the bypass flag gated by the per-executor env.
The three executors are registered in ExecutionEngine and executeTask for those kinds passes through the same gates as opencode and codex.
Arg-shape tests cover each executor plus one fake-bin smoke proving start() yields a completed result on exit 0.
